preview_text:
The model rates Mexico as a heavy favorite with a 71% win probability, leaving Czech Republic just a 7% chance of victory. This asymmetry reflects a substantial gap between the two sides in the model's assessment, positioning Mexico as the clear expected winner on a neutral ground.

The model's spread suggests a competitive encounter remains plausible but unlikely. Draw probability sits at 23%, indicating that while a stalemate is not remote, Mexico enters as the strongest outcome. The concentration of probability toward a Mexican win means the match carries low parity; Czech Republic would need a significant upset to claim three points.

A marked divergence separates the model from sportsbook consensus. Books assign Mexico only 51%, roughly 20 percentage points below the model's 71%, and grant Czech Republic 22% compared to the model's 7%. Polymarket pricing aligns closely with the sportsbook consensus. The dashboard offers no judgment on which source is more reliable; the gap is substantial enough to warrant attention from those building positions.
